relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jacob lifted his eyes and saw Esau coming with four hundred men.
He ordered the household according to fear and attachment.
Jacob went ahead and bowed seven times before his brother.
Esau ran, embraced him, kissed him, and they wept.
Esau saw the women and children, and Jacob named them as gracious gifts.
The slave-women and their children bowed.
Leah, then Joseph and Rachel, came near and bowed.
Esau asked about the gift-camps, and Jacob named favor as their purpose.
Esau said he had enough and did not need Jacob’s gift.
Jacob pressed him to receive it, saying Esau’s face had become like the face of the Divine.
Jacob gave back blessing as gift, and Esau accepted.
Esau offered to journey together.
Jacob moved at the pace of children and nursing flocks.
He asked Esau to go ahead while he traveled slowly.
Esau offered protection, but Jacob declined.
Esau returned to Seir.
Jacob made shelters at Succoth.
Jacob came in peace to Shechem and camped before the city.
He bought a piece of field from the sons of Hamor.
He raised an altar to El-Elohe-Israel, God, the Divine of Israel.
